title: Pleroma alatum
intro: Pleroma alatum, also known as the winged plectranthus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Pleroma alatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pleroma alatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.

The classification is based on morphological characteristics and molecular analysis.
